programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: A twist on the classic quiz show formula, contestants search through the depths of their knowledge in an attempt to score as few points as possible.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team, followed by weather.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: If it's got Britain talking then it will get talked about here, with stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Honey reaches out to a grief-stricken Suki. Chelsea and Gray cause havoc at his work function, and Sheree gives Lola a word of warning.

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Gregg and Chris head to Cardiff to meet Steph and Matt, parents to seven-year-old William and four-year-old Alex, with another baby on the way. Matt and Steph are stuck in a food rut due to William's serious food allergies. Following an anaphylactic reaction at the age of three, William cannot eat certain foods, including nuts, sesame seeds and flax seeds. As a result, the family are brand-loyal to particular products that they know William can safely eat.

programme.title: Our NHS: A Hidden History
programme.desc: As we emerge from a global pandemic that has turned our world upside down, David Olusoga explores the hidden history of the nurses, doctors and health workers who, for more than 70 years, have been coming to Britain from overseas to serve in the NHS. Without them the NHS would have been in danger of collapse, not least during the current COVID crisis.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: Secrets of an ISIS Smartphone
programme.desc: Our smartphones hold our secrets, the version of ourselves we may not want to show the world. With extraordinary access to the contents of a smartphone used by three British men who left their homes to go to Syria and fight with ISIS, journalist Mobeen Azhar uncovers what happened to the three men. Mobeen tries to understand their motivations, how they became radicalised and who is ultimately responsible for their one-way journey to certain death.

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Newsy chat and analysis from Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg, Chris Mason and guests.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A closer look at international news reported from London and BBC correspondents from around the world.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The BBC's Breakfast programme features the latest news, sport, business, weather and other items on a daily basis.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones present a lively mix of topical content and expert advice.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A semi in Mottingham in London, a detached house in Littleover in Derby, and a terrace in Dover in Kent are all renovated to a very high standard. The difference is the time scales to complete the work which run from under a week to more than two and a half years. Martin, Dion and Martel keep an eye on progress.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Documentaire

programme.title: Murder, Mystery & My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Leading barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass look back at their investigation into whether a young mother accused of poisoning her family in 1850 was hanged as a result of hearsay and gossip. In the mid-19th century, a poison panic gripped Victorian Britain when a slew of murders, many perpetrated by women, caused national hysteria. Their weapon of choice was an easily available powdered poison called arsenic.

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: A Welshman who relocated to Shetland twenty-three years ago is in A&E with a very sore shoulder. He fell against a fence and took out two fence-posts. Jean, the community nurse on the little archipelago of Out Skerries, is out on her rounds seeing patients and delivering medicine as a one-woman health service.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt: Animals Special
programme.desc: Eric Knowles takes a look back at some of the most interesting animal-related items bought by the red and blue teams. It can be a jungle out there, so Eric offers advice on how to spot a bargain. He also heads to Peterborough to discover an extraordinary collection of horseshoes.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: Gina's life is put in mortal danger when an attempt to lure the killer takes a deadly twist. Meanwhile, Jack makes a horrifying discovery about the killer's past, but can he get to Gina in time?

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers, David and Harry, look back at some of their most exotic Garden Rescue transformations, projects influenced by far-flung countries and cultures, from Malaysia to Zambia. They include a Caribbean garden full of tropical planting, an Eastern paradise with a centre-piece moon gate, a Japanese garden complete with a yoga deck, and a tiny backyard transformed in the style of the Mughal Empire.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Reality

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple from Newcastle who want to turn the spectacular Lake District from a holiday destination into their permanent home. With a budget of £750,000, they are looking for a property with fabulous views and enough space for visiting family. Alistair also takes to the water when he meets a passionate boat builder who restored what is thought to be the oldest passenger ferry in the world.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Business & Financial

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon and his team are in the stunning Somerset levels, working with Vicki and Bryan. The couple dreams of spending less time at work and more time with their young family by turning a corner of their field into a unique B&B.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Captain Izzie Balmer and First Mate Charlie Ross are sailing to the Isle of Wight for their final leg of this Road Trip. Charlie rips his trousers while on horseback, and Izzie ditches the classic car in favour of a speedboat.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: A twist on the classic quiz show formula, contestants search through the depths of their knowledge in an attempt to score as few points as possible.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team, followed by weather.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: If it's got Britain talking then it will get talked about here, with stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for an episode of previously unseen material from the hit comedy panel show. Among the guests joining Lee and David are Clare Balding, Liz Bonnin, Jo Brand, Asim Chaudhry, and more.

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: To teach the children to be more responsible, Lee and Lucy get a family pet.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Billy and Rocky go head-to-head, Gray lashes out at his boss, and Kheerat is concerned for Suki.

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The team is introduced to the crazy world of rock and roll when the lead singer of a band is murdered on Saint Marie. DI Goodman must keep control of the unruly suspects for long enough to solve the murder.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: A Simple Favour
programme.desc: Stephanie is a widowed, single mother who works as a vlogger in Connecticut. Her best friend, Emily, seems to have it all: a successful career, a loving family and a glamorous lifestyle. When Emily mysteriously disappears one day, Stephanie launches her own investigation by digging into her friend's past, finding a few surprises along the way.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The BBC's Breakfast programme features the latest news, sport, business, weather and other items on a daily basis.

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt and chefs Freddy Bird and Meera Sodha are joined by special guest Miles Jupp. There are great moments from the BBC food archive, including clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, Nigella Lawson and Mary Berry, and drinks expert Helen McGinn joins via video link to pick the wines to go with the studio dishes.

programme.title: Classic Mary Berry
programme.desc: Soothing and much-loved home comfort delights, from the very simple to the extremely decadent. Mary indulges her love of comfort food with homely recipes to warm the heart including eggs benedict florentine, fall-off-the-bone slow-cooked lamb shanks and deliciously rich chocolate truffle pots. She invites Swedish fire-chef Niklas Esktedt to share the joys of cave-man cooking with her, and enjoys his inventive venison meatballs with blackened celeriac and mouth-watering Swedish donuts.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A large terraced house in Earlsfield in London, ancient woodland in Eythorne in Kent and a building housing six flats in Tonypandy in Wales are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Dion and Martel meet the new owners and hear their plans which range from creating a family home for themselves, to extending a school and renovating for the rental market.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Documentaire

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: National and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha and the team are in Ardingly at the South of England Showground. The reds and blues battle it out around the fair with Tim Weeks and Caroline Hawley. Meanwhile, Natasha shows off a rather quirky find with a hidden surprise.

programme.title: Rugby League Challenge Cup Final: St Helens v Castleford
programme.desc: Mark Chapman introduces live coverage as St Helens bid to make it lucky number 13 in the Challenge Cup final against Castleford Tigers. Twelve-time winners Saints have a rich Challenge Cup pedigree, but the reigning Super League champions have not won the showpiece final since 2008. Victory here would cap a 2021 double for Saints after their women's team beat York City Knights earlier in the year at the Leigh Sports Village. Castleford's Challenge Cup wait has been even longer - they last won the tournament in 1986 - but they will be desperate to see off the Saints to give Daryl Powell the perfect send-off. Head coach Powell is leaving the Tigers at the end of the season. Will an emotionally charged afternoon end with celebrations for Castleford?

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: The Hit List Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes host this very special celebrity episode. Teaming up to play are comedian Susan Calman and Radio 1 DJ Melvin Odoom, former footballer John Barnes and Radio 1 DJ Scott Mills and football pundit and presenter Chris Kamara and Capital Radio DJ Vick Hope. The pressure is on to name as many hit songs and artists as possible to win £10,000 for their chosen charities, especially for the DJs!

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: A celebrity sport edition of the general knowledge quiz in which four teams try to come up with the answers that no-one else could think of. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and co-host Richard Osman, with contestants Tanni Grey-Thompson, David Wilkie, Matthew Hoggard, Graeme Swann, Jo Pavey, Ade Adepitan, Helen Glover and Heather Stanning.

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Olivia and Matt, a married couple from Worcester, face questions and big decisions as they bid to win money to help get a bigger house, now they have a baby daughter. But in a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen.

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Fenisha makes the ultimate choice to follow her heart, and David is distraught to realise he's destroyed his family.

programme.title: Dunkirk
programme.desc: An epic wartime action thriller written, directed and produced by Christopher Nolan. Hundreds of thousands of British and Allied troops find themselves surrounded by enemy forces in Dunkirk, northern France, during World War II. They face an impossible situation, trapped on the beach as the German troops close in. A quickly assembled British fleet leads the legendary evacuation of over 300,000 Allied military forces.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Young Guns
programme.desc: Six young outlaws in 1870s New Mexico, led by Billy the Kid, ride out to avenge the death of their mentor, a British rancher murdered by a cattle baron. The tearaways are deputised and told to bring the guilty men back alive, but Billy has other plans.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The BBC's Breakfast programme features the latest news, sport, business, weather and other items on a daily basis.

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: Andrew Marr is joined by Sajid Javid MP, health secretary, and Jonathan Ashworth MP, shadow health secretary. With news review by Victoria Newton, editor-in-chief of The Sun, and Vicki Young, BBC deputy political editor.
programme.category: Talkshow, Actualiteitenprogramma's, Nieuws, Interview, Actualiteit, Podiumkunsten

programme.title: Politics England
programme.desc: The latest political news, interviews and debate. With Steve Rotherham.

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: A thought-provoking debate and instant audience reaction on the big ethical talking points of the week, together with roving reports from around the UK.

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: The Onaivi family are well and truly at odds when to comes to where they want to spend their lives. Mum Krystyn wants to give her children the sunshine lifestyle she loved as a child by moving to Australia. But husband Joseph is more than happy with his life in the UK. A trial week in Perthgives them the chance to look at the homes, jobs and the lifestyle on offer.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles and the team are on the Welsh island of Anglesey. The reds and blues go head-to-head at the antiques fair with experts Thomas Plant and Caroline Hawley. The teams have only one hour to buy three items to sell for profit at auction.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: National and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: Songs of Praise: Arundel Cathedral
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins is in Arundel in West Sussex, home to the Dukes and Duchesses of Norfolk. She visits the Roman Catholic cathedral built by the fifteenth duke as a statement of his faith, and she explores the stunning castle gardens. Radzi Chinyanganya is invited to the Convent of the Poor Clares in Arundel to hear how they came to top the charts with their classical album, and we find out about Wheels for the World, a Christian project transforming lives by restoring wheelchairs.

programme.title: Cricket: England v Pakistan T20 Live
programme.desc: Isa Guha introduces live coverage from Headingley as England and Pakistan meet in the second of a three-match Twenty20 series. With August's Test series against India looming large, the England squad will be anxious for a strong showing in Leeds.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers compete to design a £6,000 Italian garden for a couple in Fareham. The designers' creative skills are going to be tested to the limit if the uninspiring grass patch is to become a Mediterranean idyll.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Reality

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: National and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani and Tom Heap head to Yorkshire's Flamborough Head. Anita visits England's largest onshore seabird colony at Bempton Cliffs, helping track breeding numbers and witnessing a very special arrival as a rare Albatross pays a visit. Below the cliffs, Tom explores some of the coast's chalk caves, discovering the rare life they support, and Tom and Anita are joined by local youngsters as they go on a rockpool safari to take stock of the marine life washed up at low tide.
programme.category: Natuur en milieu, Landbouw, Dieren, Reizen, Outdoor, Nieuws

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team make a second visit to Kenilworth Castle in the West Midlands, where finds include some early Meccano toys, a Special Ops radio set used behind the lines in WWII and a set of nursing medals that span three global conflicts. Fiona Bruce looks into the art of silhouette-making as an artist cuts her own portrait.

programme.title: Baptiste
programme.desc: Julien Baptiste travels to Hungary to help the British ambassador, Emma Chambers, find her missing family. When a body is found, the pressure is on to save Emma's two sons.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: National and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: The Limehouse Golem
programme.desc: Victorian London is gripped with fear as a serial killer is on the loose, leaving cryptic messages written in the blood of his victims. With few leads and increasing public pressure, Scotland Yard assigns the case to Inspector Kildare, a seasoned detective who has a sneaking suspicion that he's being set up to fail. Faced with a long list of suspects, Kildare must rely on help from a witness to stop the murders and bring the maniac to justice.

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Olivia and Matt, a married couple from Worcester, face questions and big decisions as they bid to win money to help get a bigger house, now they have a baby daughter. But in a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The BBC's Breakfast programme features the latest news, sport, business, weather and other items on a daily basis.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones present a lively mix of topical content and expert advice.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A three bedroom semi on a corner plot in Romford in Essex, a large four bedroom terrace with period features in Liverpool and a former bank in Nuneaton in Warwickshire are all sold under the hammer. All the new owners bought at auction as an investment.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Documentaire

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Leading criminal barristers Sasha Wass QC and Jeremy Dein QC revisit their investigation of an infamous Victorian poisoning case, where a wife was convicted of the murder of her husband. Liverpool, 1889. On 11 May, cotton broker James Maybrick passed away after a two-week battle with illness. A post-mortem revealed his body contained traces of the deadly poison arsenic. Suspicion soon fell on his wife, Florence Maybrick, after servants saw her handing some poisons.

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: There are serious concerns for a baby boy who arrives in A&E feeling very unwell. The team needs to make a quick decision about whether they can help him, or whether he requires specialist care 225 miles away on mainland Scotland.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross and experts Thomas Forrester and Stephanie Connell go antique-hunting in Brackley, Northamptonshire, before heading to Stratford-upon-Avon for today's auction. Charlie seeks illumination about the life of Victorian designer Charles Kempe, famous for his stained glass.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: The travelling fair comes to Brinford and the search for a runaway girl draws WPC Gina Dawson into a feud between the fair workers and a gang of Teddy boys.

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers, David and Harry, take a look at some of their favourite transformational Garden Rescues, the really tough jobs that had the biggest transformations by the team. There's a concrete back yard turned into a garden paradise with its own poppy meadow, a new build back garden that is turned into the ultimate outdoor party area and bar, and a soil-filled garden on a 45-degree slope that gets three tiers and a contemporary take on sheets of steel.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Reality

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: In England's smallest county, Rutland, a couple from Buckinghamshire with a budget of £500,000 ask Steve Brown and the team to find them a detached property with a garden big enough for a vegetable patch. Continuing the horticultural theme, Steve visits Barnsdale Gardens to see for himself the legacy left by one of the country's best loved gardeners, the late Geoff Hamilton.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Business & Financial

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon and the team are in the dramatic Black Mountains of Wales with Alex and Laura. The couple have a busy life with a craft cider business and a young family, and now they want to expand their enterprise to include unique accommodation.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The start of a brand new Road Trip with a fresh-faced expert making a debut appearance. Old hand Angus Ashworth is showing 'new boy' Steven Moore the ropes in the first of a five leg adventure. Steven is a former dealer, auctioneer and museum curator and has been valuing antiques on the telly for years. However, has he got what it takes to beat veteran Tripper Angus when it comes to finding valuable antiques? Their 1960 Hillman Super Minx fires up in the Scottish Borders, and purrs towards an auction in the Lake District at Crooklands, near Kendal. As well as lots of top treasure seeking, today's episode also includes the story of the country's greatest archers and a trip to a part of Scotland that's slowly revealing its secrets to archaeologists.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: The quiz in which contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and co-host Richard Osman.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team, followed by weather.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas are joined by Rufus Sewell, Lily Allen and former EastEnder Jake Wood, and Radio 1's Greg James, Jordan North and Vick Hope reveal where their show is going on its UK Summer Break Tour.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Tony and Nayna love being outdoors, but their new build garden in Liverpool is uninspiring. It is just grass with some planting, and the couple have run out of ideas. They are hoping for a Caribbean theme with a modern twist, and Tony dreams of a centrepiece water feature. It must have a multi-functional pergola for entertaining and for Tony to use his punchbag. Also, they want plenty of tropical planting. With a budget of £4,500, can Charlie and Lee punch above their weight?
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Reality

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Callum becomes desperate when Ben refuses to listen to his pleas. Tiffany pushes Bernie to get an answer, and Lola comes to terms with her new reality.

programme.title: The Dark Side of Horse Racing - Panorama
programme.desc: Horse racing is one of the most popular and profitable sports, a £5 billion industry in the UK and Ireland followed by millions. Panorama reporter Darragh MacIntyre investigates what can happen to racehorses when their careers end. The industry says that racing is now safer than ever, that the number of deaths on the track is falling and that the animals are looked after in retirement. Panorama discovers that off the track, many horses suffer career-ending injuries, and rather than being rehabilitated or retrained for life outside the sport, race horses that have been owned and trained by some of the biggest names in the industry have been put down, some meeting grisly deaths.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Our Race to Olympic Glory
programme.desc: As the London 2012 organisers face cynics foretelling international embarrassment, and the strategists at UK Sports try to work out how Team GB could beat the Olympic superpowers, the athletes prepare themselves for a competition that could define their legacy. Having unexpectedly come fourth in Beijing, to do even better at London meant defeating one of the three 'untouchable' sporting superpowers: the USA, China and Russia. The athletes would have to put in the performances of their lives.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: Helen Glover: The Mother of All Comebacks
programme.desc: After winning gold in Rio 2016, double Olympic champion Helen Glover decided to hang up the oars and step away from rowing to start a family. Helen had three children together with husband Steve Backshall, but the Covid-19 pandemic and ensuing lockdown brought on a change in perspective. Despite not having stepped foot in a boat since Rio, Helen decided to take a shot at a third Olympic gold.

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop are joined by guest host Steph McGovern and panellists Miles Jupp and Baroness Warsi.

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Gregg and Chris head to Cardiff to meet Steph and Matt, parents to seven-year-old William and four-year-old Alex, with another baby on the way. Matt and Steph are stuck in a food rut due to William's serious food allergies. Following an anaphylactic reaction at the age of three, William cannot eat certain foods, including nuts, sesame seeds and flax seeds. As a result, the family are brand-loyal to particular products that they know William can safely eat.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The BBC's Breakfast programme features the latest news, sport, business, weather and other items on a daily basis.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones present a lively mix of topical content and expert advice.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A terrace house in Wigston in Leicestershire, a flat in Swanscombe in Kent and a piece of land in Bolton in Lancashire are all sold under the hammer. All the developers have ambitious plans for their projects.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Documentaire

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Leading criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein revisit the troubling case of a care home nurse convicted of poisoning a patient, a crime for which she would hang. Nottingham, 1935. Ada Baguley, a 55-year-old resident of a family-run nursing home, passed away. Initially Ada was thought to have died from natural causes, but a post-mortem revealed her body to contain lethal doses of morphine.

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Paramedics and the coastguard rescue team help a six foot nine inch Norwegian nicknamed Tiny off his tall ship. The sailor was on his way to the Shetland Folk Festival, but having a bad ankle fracture means he'll spend the night in hospital instead.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The team are at The Highland Centre in Edinburgh. Natasha Raskin Sharp and the teams are joined by experts Ben Cooper and Tim Weeks. The reds and blues race around the fair hoping to bag themselves three items that will hopefully make them a profit at auction. Meanwhile Natasha pays a visit to The Jupiter Artland sculpture park in Edinburgh.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: While Max and Fenton investigate a flood of counterfeit notes, is it too much of a coincidence that pickpocket Brody's luck has changed?

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: This time, Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers, David and Harry, look back at some of their favourite inspirational Garden Rescues, the ones that draw inspiration from special memories. This includes a garden transformation with memories of a honeymoon in the South of France, a New York skyline inspired garden designed by Arit, a Moroccan paradise, and a space inspired by the Lost Gardens of Heligan.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Reality

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah is in Devon, accompanying a married couple from Berkshire with an impressive budget of £1,200,000. On today's property shopping list is a five-bedroom detached property with land where their four children can enjoy the great outdoors. Away from the house hunt, Sonali visits a farm where she discovers that flowers are not the only way to her heart. Edible flowers are too.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Business & Financial

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: In the Forest of Dean, Simon and the team meet semi-retired couple Becky and David. Now that their children have flown the nest, they're keen for a new adventure and want to set up a small glamping business in their peaceful and picturesque garden.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Steven Moore and Angus Ashworth, plus their shiny, white Hillman Super Minx called `Betty', are shopping their way along the byways of Scotland. Yorkshireman Angus bags something aromatic and coffee related, while Geordie new boy Steven plumps for a little treasure from his fellow tripper's back yard in Yorkshire.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: A twist on the classic quiz show formula, contestants search through the depths of their knowledge in an attempt to score as few points as possible.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team, followed by weather.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas are joined by star of `Motherland' and `Line of Duty' Anna Maxwell Martin and her new co-star Hollywood funny man Chris O'Dowd. They will be talking about their West End play `Constellations'. Lucy Siegle looks at the worrying trend of plastics in our clothes and we catch up with the kidnapped Greg James in his camper van, somewhere in the UK.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The Panesars try to stick together for Jags's funeral, and Callum fears for Ben.

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Dom is shocked when he discovers Carole's secret, Hanssen is torn as to how to handle Abs's secret, and Josh tries to prove to Ange that he's up to the role of being a father.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon is back in the host's chair with Lee Mack and David Mitchell returning as the team captains. Sorting the fact from the fiction this week are guests Les Dennis, Richard Osman, Alice Levine and Lou Sanders.

programme.title: Uprising
programme.desc: In the early hours of 18 January 1981, in a house in south London, a birthday party ended in a fire. Thirteen young black British people died. The fire and its aftermath would ignite an uprising by the black British community. This film tells the stories of the young people who were at the party and the events that led up to it.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: Flat Out Fabulous
programme.desc: Whinnie and Tyler meet seven housemates who are desperate to give their digs a new lease of life. Find out if Whinnie can pull off a Roaring Twenties cinema theme for the cost of their big night out.

programme.title: Flat Out Fabulous
programme.desc: Cardiff calls, and Whinnie's not the only queen in town. She and Tyler meet three housemates: Leon, Callum and Rhys, also known as Leonce, Malady Masterwort and Just Becky, drag queens who live together. The trio love living in Cardiff for its great nightlife and the hustle and bustle of living in the capital. They are big personalities, but their living space does not represent them.

programme.title: Fast Food Face-Off
programme.desc: Stand-up comedian Josh Jones pits Kristian Nairn and Gemma Bradley against each other in a competition to find the best takeaway in town. Host Josh Jones loves to eat amazing food but, because of his busy work and travel schedule as a stand-up comedian, he usually ends up living off takeaways. But that doesn't mean his tastebuds have to be disappointed, he likes his fast food to be fancy.

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Caleb remains chained in the basement as Chick starts to make himself useful in the Bates house. Madeleine arrives to apologise for the disastrous dinner and explains that things are strained between her and Sam.

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: As Chick disposes of Caleb's body, Norman is visited by Sheriff Jane Greene who questions him about Jim Blackwell. Blackwell has skipped bail, and the motel's address has been found amongst his possessions.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The BBC's Breakfast programme features the latest news, sport, business, weather and other items on a daily basis.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones present a lively mix of topical content and expert advice.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A terrace house in Stourbridge in the Midlands, a terrace house in Alkrington in Manchester and a semi in Wigan in Lancashire are all sold under the hammer. All three properties are bought by developers who have spotted money making potential.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Documentaire

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Top criminal barristers Sasha Wass QC and Jeremy Dein QC revisit their investigation into an infamous case of a housewife accused of murdering her young husband. She became the last woman to be publicly hanged in Dorset, and her story inspired a classic novel. Dorset, 1856. In the early hours of 6 July, Martha Brown, a resident of Birdsmoorgate in rural Dorset, called on her neighbour begging for help.

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: An oil rig worker from Tyne and Wear is rushed to A&E with a suspected stroke, and a patient makes a four hundred and fifty mile round trip for a hospital appointment that takes just twenty minutes.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles presents from an antiques centre in Cheshire, with some changes to the rules: with the help of Ben Cooper and Richard Madley, one of the items bought by each team must cost at least £75 and they must also buy an item of Eric's choosing.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: An undercover operation for Fenton threatens to expose a secret that could ruin Inspector Briggs and Susie takes matters into her own hands when Tommy is bullied by the other officers.

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers, David and Harry, look back at some of their favourite eco Garden Rescues: the ones that help Mother Nature. There is a garden that features a hedgehog highway for rescued hedgehogs in Andover, Arit builds a garden to attract bees, taking her design cues from beehives, and a battered old shed gets the ultimate recycled makeover from the team. There's also plenty of brilliant new gardening tips from Charlie and the boys.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Reality

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple from Bedfordshire with a budget of £450,000 need Ginny Buckley's help in Norfolk to find them the first house they are buying together. They would like a character property with a good-sized garden for some new canine companions. Ginny also heads to Great Yarmouth, where she straps in for a white-knuckle ride on one of the country's last remaining wooden rollercoasters.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Business & Financial

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Mel and Tom live in the oldest house in their North Somerset village of Wrington. They are both writers, but they admit they don't have any creativity when it comes to interior design.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: It's the halfway point of our treasure trip, and experts Angus Ashworth and Steven Moore are still having fun in Bonnie Scotland. On the way to a saleroom back below the border in Clitheroe, they discover the well-spring of water polo and enjoy a very soggy Highland Games.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: A twist on the classic quiz show formula, contestants search through the depths of their knowledge in an attempt to score as few points as possible.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team, followed by weather.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Dwayne Johnson and Emily Blunt join Alex Jones and Ronan Keating to talk about their new Disney film `Jungle Cruise'. In Watchdog, Matt Allwright investigates one of the UK's biggest car manufacturers.

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: First through the doors is Annalisa, with a unique piece of farming history. Also facing the dealers are mother and daughters Jennifer, Ann and Gina, with a fascinating tribal mask bought from their local school.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: First, art conservator Lucia Scalisi faces the thrilling task of restoring a rare 17th-century painting of Queen Henrietta Maria, the wife of Charles I. For owner Howard Bird, the painting is invaluable as it represents a lifeline between himself and a dear friend who sadly passed away. As Lucia scales away the layers of dark overpaint, she makes a discovery.

programme.title: Uprising
programme.desc: The story of the aftermath of the New Cross fire and the run up to the Black People's Day of Action. As news spread about the fire at 439 New Cross Road, the scale of the tragedy overwhelmed the local community. Amid uncertainty about whether the fire had been caused by a racist firebomb attack, anger mounted at the police investigation and the seeming indifference of the press and the government to the loss of so many black lives.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: Jade Jones: Fighting for Gold
programme.desc: An intimate portrait of double Olympic taekwondo gold medalist Jade Jones as she prepares to try to make history by becoming the first British woman to win gold medals in three successive games. The film offers a highly personal insight into the life of the tough, competitive and uncompromising girl from Flint. Jade, her friends, her family and her trainers reveal how a troubled youth dominated by shoplifting and problems at school was transformed through her love of sport.

programme.title: Sir Chris Hoy's Secret World of Japanese Bicycle Racing
programme.desc: Olympic icon Sir Chris Hoy heads to Japan, host of the 2021 Tokyo Games, to explore the phenomenon of the keirin, the most extreme and exciting event in track cycling. A test of controlled pacing then a tactical fight to the line, keirin is a sport in its own right in its country of origin and is steeped in the history and rituals that make Japanese culture so unique.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Our Race to Olympic Glory
programme.desc: As the London 2012 organisers face cynics foretelling international embarrassment, and the strategists at UK Sports try to work out how Team GB could beat the Olympic superpowers, the athletes prepare themselves for a competition that could define their legacy. Having unexpectedly come fourth in Beijing, to do even better at London meant defeating one of the three 'untouchable' sporting superpowers: the USA, China and Russia. The athletes would have to put in the performances of their lives.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The BBC's Breakfast programme features the latest news, sport, business, weather and other items on a daily basis.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones present a lively mix of topical content and expert advice.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A terrace house in Hornchurch in Essex, a piece of land in Newquay in Cornwall and a commercial space in Rochdale in Lancashire are all sold under the hammer. Plans vary from selling on for a profit to creating something for the community as well as a family home.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Documentaire

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Top criminal barristers Jeremy Dein QC and Sasha Wass QC revisit the complex case against a Victorian auctioneer accused alongside his brother and two others of murdering his wife. Penge, 1877. On 12 April a desperately frail woman was taken on an arduous journey from rural Kent to Penge on the outskirts of south east London. That woman was 36-year-old wealthy heiress Harriett Staunton. Moaning and barely conscious, she had to be carried the final stretch to her lodging house.

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Nurse Chloe starts her first shift as a fully-qualified nurse and is soon in at the deep end, as a toddler arrives having difficulty breathing. The team swing into action to try to understand why she's ill and whether an air ambulance is needed.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt: Homes and Interiors Special
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp looks back at some decorative buys from past episodes, presenting the show from a beautiful country manor house in Scotland. Charlie Ross learns all about the craft of Welsh blankets, and Eric Knowles visits Liverpool's St Georges Hall to admire its incredible interior design.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: Coulson's sexual advances on Gina take a sinister turn, Fenton's corruption is exposed and Swift becomes obsessed with Susie's new beau.

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers, David and Harry, look back at some of the brothers' favourite Garden Rescues. There's designs from the boys, but Charlie gets a look-in too. There are two gardens in Manchester that become one, complete with an epic Scandinavian influenced makeover, a tiny back-garden that is transformed into a wildlife paradise which maximises every bit of space, and an awkwardly sloped garden that gets two tiers.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Reality

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Steve Brown showcases some tempting properties to a young couple of keen cyclists who have a budget of £600,000 to make the move from London to the beautiful countryside of North Yorkshire. Steve also meets a truly inspirational, award-winning cheese maker from Syria who's brought a taste of home to the British countryside.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Business & Financial

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: In rural Oxfordshire, Simon Parfett and his team meet Asiyla and her family. They live in a beautiful spot where the three generations of her family live and farm together. Among the free-roaming geese sits an old sailing boat.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Angus and Steven visit a couple of venerable survivors from the Industrial Revolution and enjoy yet more delicious indigenous pastry products, before heading off to an exciting auction in the North Yorkshire town of Bedale.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: A twist on the classic quiz show formula, contestants search through the depths of their knowledge in an attempt to score as few points as possible.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team, followed by weather.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Frankie's nerves overcome her, Bobby gets a surprise and wonders if Dana has been honest, and Callum fears the worst when he learns the truth.

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin are in Windsor with single mum Holly, who has her hands full looking after her two sons, Spencer, 16, and Fletcher, 11. Caring for Spencer, who has cerebral palsy, leaves Holly little time to cook, and as a result the family have become dependent on microwavable ready meals. Whilst Spencer is happy to eat most foods, Fletcher who has recently been diagnosed with autism, is fussier. He regularly skips meals and, like his mum, relies on sugary snacks to get him through the day. It's time for this family's diet to have a nutritional overhaul. Can Gregg and Chris get the family cooking tasty food from scratch and tempt them away from their favourite big brands in order to save enough for a much-needed holiday?Gregg teaches Holly three simple egg recipes she can quickly whip up for the boys after school, whilst Chris gets Fletcher baking breakfast muffins to set him up for the day and hopefully reduce his snacking. A group from the Wiltshire Wildlife Trust te.

programme.title: Uprising
programme.desc: After the New Cross fire and the Black People's Day of Action, tensions between the community and the police escalated when a massive stop-and-search operation was launched, targeting black people on the streets of Brixton. In April, the situation boiled over into one of the biggest riots in British history. Buildings were burned down and hundreds of police injured. Riots then flared up all over the country, from Southall to Toxteth, but by the year's end, the people of New Cross were no closer to knowing who started the New Cross fire or why - and a lack of answers and justice has lingered over the case ever since.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: The Rap Game UK is back in a new city, Manchester. Six artists from across the UK compete against each other for the chance to win a £20k music prize. In episode one, straight after meeting one another, the artists perform for the first time in front of rap legends Krept, Konan and DJ Target. In a cypher performance all rapping on the same beat, the six must intro themselves - but not all rise to the challenge. Later, after being blown away by the luxury penthouse in the heart of Manchester in which they'll live and work for the duration of the process, the artists discover their next challenge - to write about one day that changed their life. Special guest Russ Millions watches and critiques before the artists discover their first week's rankings, but not all are happy with the result.

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Newsy chat and analysis from Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg, Chris Mason and guests.

programme.title: Our NHS: A Hidden History
programme.desc: As we emerge from a global pandemic that has turned our world upside down, David Olusoga explores the hidden history of the nurses, doctors and health workers who, for more than 70 years, have been coming to Britain from overseas to serve in the NHS. Without them the NHS would have been in danger of collapse, not least during the current COVID crisis.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The BBC's Breakfast programme features the latest news, sport, business, weather and other items on a daily basis.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones present a lively mix of topical content and expert advice.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A three bed semi in Darwen in Lancashire, a plot of land in Cromford in Derbyshire and an end terrace in Chatham in Kent are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Dion and Martel talk to the developers and follow progress as they work on their auction buys.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Documentaire

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: Olympics 2020: Opening Ceremony
programme.desc: Live coverage from Tokyo's National Stadium, where, after a delay of a year, the games of the thirty-second Olympiad will finally be declared open and the Olympic flame will be lit. The global pandemic and the suffering it has caused means that the ceremony will be simpler than had been originally envisaged, but it is sure to still be a spectacular and moving showpiece as Japan welcomes the athletes of the world. Presented by Clare Balding and Alex Scott, with ceremony commentary from Hazel Irvine and Andrew Cotter.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton guides a couple who want to switch their lives from the bustle of Bristol to the fresh country air of the Wye Valley on a budget of £300,000. While exploring the River Wye, Alistair also discovers the slippery challenges of sustainably sourcing and restocking the European eel.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Business & Financial

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Steven Moore and Angus Ashworth are out and about in the North East of England. The extremely circuitous open road having somehow taken hundreds of miles to get there from nearby Melrose in the Scottish Borders. There will be Whitby jet, the sheep of the beautiful North York Moors and even a trip back north to Dundee.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: A twist on the classic quiz show formula, contestants search through the depths of their knowledge in an attempt to score as few points as possible.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team, followed by weather.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: If it's got Britain talking then it will get talked about here, with stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: Olympics 2020: Opening Ceremony Highlights
programme.desc: Highlights of the opening ceremony, which took place at Tokyo's National Stadium, signalling the start of the 2020 Olympic Games.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany's actions backfire and she is left mortified. Sonia plans to spend some quality time with her dad, and Zack has a decision to make.

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The team is introduced to the crazy world of rock and roll when the lead singer of a band is murdered on Saint Marie. DI Goodman must keep control of the unruly suspects for long enough to solve the murder.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: The Lost Boys
programme.desc: Fang-in-cheek tale about a single-parent family who move to the coastal town of Santa Clara where the local juvenile delinquents are actually a pack of vampires.

programme.title: Olympics 2020
programme.desc: JJ Chalmers presents all the action as the 2020 Olympics finally get under way, with the first medals presented, and early British interest in a multitude of events. We'll see the beginning of the men's cycling road race, while in rowing, it's the heats of the men's coxless four, with Britain hoping for a sixth consecutive gold in the event. And the first gold medal of the games will go to the winner of the women's 10m air rifle.

programme.title: Olympics 2020
programme.desc: The first day of the 2020 Olympics continues with the action including the men's road race, where GB's team is stacked with Grand Tour winners. Presented by Dan Walker and Sam Quek.

programme.title: Olympic Breakfast
programme.desc: Team GB's men's road cyclists go for gold, while Max Whitlock is among the British gymnasts competing for places in both team and individual finals in Tokyo. Britain's four-man road race team in Tokyo includes three Grand Tour champions, including Tour de France winner Geraint Thomas, Vuelta winner Simon Yates and Giro d'Italia winner Tao Geoghegan Hart. Whitlock famously won double Olympic gold in Rio and will be aiming for more medals in Tokyo.

programme.title: Olympics 2020 Live
programme.desc: Adam Peaty begins his campaign to become the first Briton to retain an Olympic swimming title, while the men's road race champion will be crowned in the shadow of Mount Fuji. Peaty has dominated the men's 100m breaststroke since winning the Olympic title in Rio, and became the first man to break the 57-second barrier at the 2019 World Championships. Earlier this year, he retained his European title, and he will be looking to lay down another marker in today's heats. Elsewhere, Team GB's men's hockey team begin their Olympic campaign against South Africa. Presented by Hazel Irvine and Gabby Logan.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: National and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: Olympics 2020 Live
programme.desc: Hazel Irvine and Gabby Logan introduce the latest action from the opening day of the Olympics, with medals decided in fencing, judo and taekwondo. Britain's Ashley McKenzie is looking for a medal in the men's -60kg judo, while the men's gymnastics qualifying continues, with Max Whitlock and his GB team-mates looking to book places in the team final and in the individual apparatus finals.

programme.title: Olympics 2020
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad recaps the best of the action from the opening day of the Olympics, including a round-up of the hockey and football. There will also be highlights from 3x3 basketball, which is making its debut at these Games.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: National and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: Take Off with Bradley & Holly
programme.desc: Gameshow hosted by Bradley Walsh and Holly Willoughby where members of the public compete to win seats on a plane to LA and Las Vegas.

programme.title: Olympics 2020: Today at the Games
programme.desc: Clare Balding, Alex Scott and guests look back over the opening day of the Olympics, with reports and reaction on all the big stories. The first medals of the games were awarded in shooting, while the men's cycling road race winner was crowned after a gruelling 234km course that included a stretch on the slopes of Mount Fuji. Max Whitlock and his British team-mates were competing in the men's team gymnastics, while medals were also awarded in archery, fencing, judo, shooting and taekwondo.

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Three contestants hope to answer their way to a fortune, guided by seven celebrities. Celebrities include Clare Balding, Anton Du Beke, Edith Bowman, Rob Rinder, Lady Leshurr, Dr Xand Van Tulleken and Alex Beresford.

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jacob struggles to find a safe path as tensions with Tina escalate.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Lady Bird
programme.desc: Critically acclaimed comedy drama about a high school student in early 2000s Sacremento as she navigates the tricky world around her and prepares for college. Upset that her poor family insist on sending her to private school - and with her mother believing she is showing a lack of gratitude - the domestic dramas come thick and fast as she also contends with romance, friendships and studies - all the while dreaming of getting the grades that will allow her to escape to a top university.

programme.title: Olympics 2020 Live
programme.desc: British Olympic champions Adam Peaty and Jade Jones are both in action on day two, which also includes skateboarding, an exciting new addition to the games. Presented by JJ Chalmers. Reigning 100m breaststroke champion Peaty will be aiming to book his place in the final, while other swimming highlights include Japanese star Daiya Seto going for gold at his home games in the 400m individual medley. Meanwhile, Britain's Jade Jones begins her quest to become the first taekwondo fighter to win three Olympic gold medals. Team-mate and fellow world champion Bradly Sinden will also be competing. Two years ago, the Doncaster fighter became the first British man to win gold at the World Championships. Key finals: 0230 Men's 400m IM final 0254 Men's 400m freestyle final 0312 Women's 400m IM final 0342 Women's 4x100m freestyle final 0430-0528 Skateboarding - men's street final.

programme.title: Olympic Breakfast
programme.desc: Multiple Olympic gold medallists Simone Biles and Jade Jones are among the stars competing as day two gets into its stride in Tokyo. Double taekwondo champion Olympic Jones will expect to be competing in the semi-finals, where a win would guarantee another medal, while Biles will be in action in the qualifying for the team and individual apparatus finals. Meanwhile, Britain's London 2012 silver medallist Lizzie Deignan will be riding in the women's road race.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: National and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: National and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: As more and more of us yearn for a life in the country, Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith roll up their sleeves on a community farm in Herefordshire. Matt helps to save a bee colony and harvests fruits from their walled garden. Charlotte hand milks Snowdrop, one of the community's dairy cows, before making her own butter. It's the final challenge for Dwayne Fields and his gallant team of adventurers as they set out to climb Wales's biggest mountain, Snowdon.
programme.category: Natuur en milieu, Landbouw, Dieren, Reizen, Outdoor, Nieuws

programme.title: Olympics 2020: Today at the Games
programme.desc: Clare Balding and Alex Scott look back at the main events of day two, in which Jade Jones and Bradly Sinden were hoping to make taekwondo history. British cyclist Lizzie Deignan was competing in the women's road race, while Adam Peaty and Katie Ledecky were among those in action in the swimming pool. Peaty is a hot favourite to retain his title in the 100m breaststroke, while Ledecky is aiming to add to her tally of five Olympics golds amassed in London and Rio.

programme.title: Baptiste
programme.desc: Julien and Zsofia track down the tattooed man but he gives little away. Things escalate when the police receive a hostage video, and Emma takes drastic action for her family.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: National and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: Olympic Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Sam Quek guide you through the early day-three action, which includes Tom Daley and Matty Lee on the hunt for diving medals in the men's 10m synchro. Daley, competing in his fourth Olympics, won bronze in this event in Rio alongside Daniel Goodfellow while he and Lee took bronze together at the 2019 World Championships. The medals will be decided in the men's C1 canoe slalom and archery men's team event, while there will also be taekwondo and men's rugby sevens action.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team, followed by weather.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: If it's got Britain talking then it will get talked about here, with stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: Olympics 2020: Today at the Games
programme.desc: Clare Balding and Alex Scott look back at the main events of day three, where Adam Peaty was hoping to retain his men's 100m breaststroke title, one of 21 golds up for grabs. American swimmer Katie Ledecky had the first of her potential five golds in her sights, while Jonny Brownlee and Alex Yee were bidding for a third consecutive British gold in the men's triathlon. Medals were also decided in a host of sports including gymnastics, taekwondo and skateboarding.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mick finds out the truth and makes a decision, Karen has a plan to sort out a rift between her kids, and Ruby leans on Jean for support instead of Martin.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Joining Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell for this week's tall tales are guests Stephen Hendry, Laura Whitmore, Chris McCausland and Maisie Adam.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: Olympic Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Sam Quek guide you through the early day-four action, where British divers look to make history. Eden Cheng and Lois Toulson hope to compete in the women's 10m synchro diving final, where they could become the first British women to win an Olympic medal since 1960. Kimberley Woods targets a medal in the women's K1 canoe slalom in which Australia's Jessica Fox will be looking to make amends for Rio, where she had been the big favourite but had had to settle for bronze.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team, followed by weather.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: If it's got Britain talking then it will get talked about here, with stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: Olympics 2020: Today at the Games
programme.desc: Clare Balding and Alex Scott present highlights and talking points from day four of the Olympics, where there were 22 golds to be won, with Britain hoping to grab at least one. Triple world champion Bianca Walkden aimed to land her first Olympic title, American gymnast Simone Biles had a fifth in her sights, and the GB dressage riders also aimed for the podium.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ruby makes Jean an offer, Chelsea tries to prove Karen wrong, and as Linda stands by Nancy, Frankie's guilt worsens.

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Holby City's new director of improvement makes plans to turn the hospital around, and Ange puts her personal news aside and tries to prove that she runs a tight ship on AAU.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: Flat Out Fabulous
programme.desc: Megan, Jen and Fran are a Geordie trio who have bonded over their love of animals. Between them they own two rabbits, a hamster, three rats and a cat called Poodle. The animals brought them together and the girls have lived together for eight months. They are ready to ditch the orange walls and tatty sofas in favour of a bright space that they can entertain and hang out in. Their vision is glitzy glamour but fellow animal lover Whinnie has a very different vision.

programme.title: Flat Out Fabulous
programme.desc: Saski, Jess, Daniella and Tom love living in Cambridge for its amazing architecture and impressive pub scene. What's not so impressive is their lacklustre living room. The girls all work for the NHS and have had a particularly difficult time during the pandemic, so Whinnie and Tyler hope to redesign their space to give them a room that they can chill in after a long shift, but also use as a party space to let off some steam.

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop are joined by guest host Richard Ayoade and panellists Roisin Conaty and Armando Iannucci.

programme.title: Olympic Breakfast
programme.desc: Britain's Jack Laugher and Dan Goodfellow dive for gold in the men's 3m synchro. Laugher defends the title he won with Chris Mears in Rio, and he and Goodfellow took silver at the last World Championships, so will be hoping to go one better in Japan. Plus coverage of the conclusion of the men's road time trial, while Britain's Mallory Franklin begins her medal quest in the heats of the women's C1 canoe slalom.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team, followed by weather.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: If it's got Britain talking then it will get talked about here, with stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: Olympics 2020: Today at the Games
programme.desc: Clare Balding and Alex Scott present highlights of a thrilling day's action from Tokyo 2020 in which Britain's Charlotte Dujardin went in search of yet another games gold. Dujardin had a hat-trick on her mind in the individual dressage while elsewhere in Tokyo it was all about collective glory. In rowing, Britain's men's four attempted to continue a run that stretches back to Sydney 2000 by winning a sixth-consecutive gold medal.

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould head to the Norfolk countryside on the trail of one of the 20th century's greatest artists, Henry Moore. They are investigating an intriguing sculpture that belongs to retired dairy farmer Neil Betts and his wife Barbara, who, over the years, have used the piece as a doorstop and even a water feature, when Neil draped a hosepipe over it. t was only when a friend suggested it looked very much like the work of Henry Moore that the Betts began to see similarities.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: Radio 1 Live Lounge Special
programme.desc: To be advised.

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Three contestants hope to answer their way to a fortune, guided by seven celebrities. Celebrities include Clare Balding, Anton Du Beke, Edith Bowman, Rob Rinder, Lady Leshurr, Dr Xand Van Tulleken and Alex Beresford.

programme.title: Olympic Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Sam Quek present live coverage as Britain's Mallory Franklin hopes to upstage her Australian rival Jessica Fox and claim canoe slalom glory in Tokyo. Franklin will be in action in the C1 final, a new event for Tokyo but one in which Franklin is a former world champion. Elsewhere, Quek, an Olympic hockey gold medallist at Rio 2016, will no doubt be keen to see how Britain's men get on against the Netherlands in what could be a crucial group match.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.
